\poemtitle{In Response, A Sonnet}


\begin{verse}
Watch hunters draw the elephant to die, \\
\hspace{2em}in love with some false woman they have made, \\
a    mask of grace, a mistress-shaped facade, \\
bright, fatal, dressed beneath a painted eye.
\end{verse}
\vspace{1.2ex}
\begin{verse}
If human wit runs subtler, then, than I \\
\hspace{2em}can name in any beast that God has made, \\
\hspace{2em}who blames me if I fall upon this blade \\
and die for you, not forced, but willingly?
\end{verse}
\vspace{1.2ex}
\begin{verse}
Some will condemn me; let them. They have known \\
\hspace{2em}no sight of you, no angel in the face, \\
no sovereign form that alters blood and breath;
\end{verse}
\vspace{1.2ex}
\begin{verse}
nor felt how beauty makes its empire shown, \\
\hspace{2em}nor passed beneath her wide imperial grace, \\
nor loved, nor stood so near a living death.
\end{verse}







\poemtitle{No Serrana}


\begin{verse}
I have heard men \\
compare women to spring. \\
Loose habit. \\
Dead speech.
\end{verse}
\vspace{1.2ex}
\begin{verse}
I asked \\
\textit{donde estaba?} \\
\textit{Where is the cowherd girl?}
\end{verse}
\vspace{1.2ex}
\begin{verse}
She smiled \\
like someone hearing \\
the wrong man \\
knock on the right door.
\end{verse}
\vspace{1.2ex}
\begin{verse}
She said \\
``that girl wants no part of love.'' \\
Meaning herself. \\
\textit{esa moza no quiere} \\
\textit{saber nada de amores.}
\end{verse}
\vspace{1.2ex}
\begin{verse}
She meant, in fact said: \\
go back to your saddle.
\end{verse}







\poemtitle{The Marques Dreams of Her}


\begin{verse}
Dido at Carthage? \\
Or the same girl, buying carnations \\
at a market in California.
\end{verse}
\vspace{1.2ex}
\begin{verse}
Are you still real? \\
still moving? \\
Moving beyond my eyes \\
that found you.
\end{verse}
\vspace{1.2ex}
\begin{verse}
Your image, your ghostly presence, \\
divine enough, yes, \\
to deliver the wound --- \\
or not.
\end{verse}
\vspace{1.2ex}
\begin{verse}
A rusting arrow in the chest. \\
The dart that pierces me \\
\& marks me also blessed.
\end{verse}
\vspace{1.2ex}
\begin{verse}
The one that makes me glad, \\
content, \& full of grief, \\
whispers: \\
``Let me dress the wound.''
\end{verse}
\vspace{1.2ex}
\begin{verse}
I move through unearned pain, \\
burning, strangely at ease. \\
Having confessed, \\
I can almost rest.
\end{verse}
